{{ill|Q11557453|lt=Q11557453|WD=Q11557453}} {{commons category|Category:Sakitama-jinja}} [[JA:前玉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11397359}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakitama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakitama-jinja, haiden.JPG|250px]]
Approach and worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Gyōda|Q860659}}{{jalink|Ōaza|Q424857}} Sakitama {{jalink|Koaza|Q5327509|Aza}} Miyamae 5450 |位置 = {{Coord|36|7|23.5|N|139|28|46.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto
Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (2 minor seats)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Sakitama Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|April 15|Q2497}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=7|lat_sec=23.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=28|lon_sec=46.7|label=Sakitama Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{Coord|36|7|23.5|N|139|28|46.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Sengen-zuka Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|{{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}} where Sakitama Shrine is enshrined}}]] '''Sakitama Shrine''' (さきたまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sakitama, {{jalink|Gyōda|Q860659}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534|shrine rank}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. The shrine name “Sakitama” is said to be the origin of the place name “Saitama”Shrine history board.. It stands within the {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}} in northern Saitama Prefecture, and the shrine itself is on top of {{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following two. * '''{{jalink|Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Sakitamahime|Q0|Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto}}''' : In the '{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}},' regarding Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto, it is written that she is a daughter of {{jalink|Ame-no-Mikushi-no-Kami}} and consort of {{jalink|Hayamikatake-sawayajinu-mi-no-Kami}}, a descendant of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-Ōkami}} (there is no description regarding Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto). === History === === Founding === The founding is unknown. Sakitama Shrine stands within the {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}}, and its main sanctuary also faces the direction of the kofun cluster. The ruling stratum of these kofun is estimated to have been the ancient ritual group of Sakitama Shrine{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}, and one theory infers that worship began in the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}} as protection of the kofun cluster. The origin of the shrine name “Sakitama” is also unclear, but because the family register ledger “Yamashiro no Kuni Atago-gun Kumoshimo no Sato Keichō” (in the '{{jalink|Shōsōin Documents|Q11545362}}') from {{jalink|Jinki|Q1373210}} 3 ({{jalink|726|Q23555}}) already shows the notation “Musashi no Kuni Sakitama-gun,” this “Sakitama” is thought to have later changed to “Saitama (sakitama/saitama)”. === Overview history === In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is recorded in {{jalink|Sakitama District|Q11427160}} of {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}} as “Sakitama Shrine, two seats,” and both seats are listed as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Both readings “Sakitama” and “Saitama” are annotated. Movements in the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}} are unknown{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}, but according to shrine tradition, after inviting an Asama shrine from {{jalink|Oshi Castle|Q1074393}}, it took the name “Asama Shrine,” and the kofun where it stands was regarded as Mt. Fuji{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}. It is also said that originally the shrine on top of the kofun was called “Upper Shrine” and the one on the middle slope “Lower Shrine,” and in Meiji this was fixed as “Sakitama Shrine” (upper) and “Asama Shrine” (lower){{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} in the modern shrine rank system. === Precincts === [[File:Sakitama-jinja, tourou.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{Center|Man'yō lanterns (both sides of approach)
(Gyōda City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Sakitama-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{Center|Stone torii (Gyōda City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Sakitama-jinja, maki.JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{Center|[[マキ|Maki tree]] (Gyōda City-designated Natural Monument)}}]] The main shrine buildings stand on the summit of {{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}} and consist of main sanctuary and worship hall. On both sides of the stair entrance to the summit stand two 2-meter stone lanterns called “Man'yō Lanterns.” They were reportedly dedicated by parishioners of Sakitama Shrine on October 15, {{jalink|1697|Q7702}} ({{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} 10) to commemorate fulfillment of prayers. Each lantern bears one poem from the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' related to this area. {{Cquote| '''Sakitama no Tsu''' (Vol.14, No.3380, anonymous) * Man'yōgana text : 佐吉多万能　津爾乎流布禰乃　可是乎伊多美 : 都奈波多由登毛　許登奈多延曽禰 * Reading : 埼玉（さきたま）の　津に居（を）る船の　風をいたみ : 綱は絶ゆとも　言（こと）な絶えそね '''Kosaki-numa''' (Vol.9, No.1744, anonymous) * Man'yōgana text : 前玉之　小埼乃沼爾　鴨曽翼霧 : 己尾爾　零置流霜乎　掃等爾有欺 * Reading : 埼玉の　小埼の沼に　鴨そ翼（はね）霧（き）る : 己（おの）が尾に　降りおける霜を　払ふとにあらし }} These lanterns are designated cultural properties of Gyōda City. The “{{jalink|Kosaki-numa|Q18991228}}” in the poem has an identified location east of Sakitama Shrine ({{Coord|36|07|13.24|N|139|30|06.51|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|name=Kosaki-numa}}). Also, the stone torii at the precinct entrance was built in November {{jalink|1676|Q7628}} ({{jalink|Enpō|Q1145735}} 4) and is designated a cultural property of Gyōda City. The {{jalink|maki|Q117435}} tree standing near the torii is estimated at 600 years old and 16 meters tall, the largest existing maki in Saitama Prefecture, and is designated a natural monument of Gyōda City. === Sessha and massha === * Asama Shrine ** Deity: {{jalink|Konohanasakuyahime|Q80497|Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto}} *: Said to have been invited from {{jalink|Oshi Castle|Q1074393}}. Before Meiji, the main shrine on the kofun summit was called “Upper Shrine,” and Asama Shrine on the slope was called “Lower Shrine.” * Meiji Shrine : Shrines of the Sakitama district were merged in the {{jalink|Meiji period}}. Total of 16 deities. * Tenjin Shrine * Ebisu Shrine File:Sakitama-jinja, Sengen-jinja.JPG|Asama Shrine File:Sakitama-jinja, Meiji-jinja.JPG|Meiji Shrine === Festivals === List of annual festivals held at Sakitama Shrine. * {{jalink|New Year Festival}} ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * Selection rite for sacred rice field, opening rite for sacred rice field work (late March) * Kangaku Festival (early April) * {{jalink|Grand Annual Festival}} ({{jalink|April 15|Q2497}}) * Rice-planting rite (early June) * Hatsuyama ({{jalink|July 1|Q2700}}-{{jalink|July 15|Q2686|15}}) * {{jalink|Nagoshi no Ōharae}} ({{jalink|July 30|Q2716}}) * Tanabata rite (early August) * Harvest-cutting festival (early October) * Lantern Festival ({{jalink|October 15|Q2919}}) * {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}} (late October) * {{jalink|Shichi-Go-San|Q1072387}} (late October - November) * {{jalink|Hoshi Festival}} (winter solstice in December) * {{jalink|Ōharae|Q11261910}} ({{jalink|December 31|Q2912}}) * Sakitama rite (December 31) === Cultural properties === === Gyōda City-designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone torii (building) - designated March 25, 1999[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/sakitamajinjyanoisitorii.html Stone torii of Sakitama Shrine] (Gyōda City website).. ** Stone lanterns (crafts) - designated March 10, 1967[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/isidourou.html Stone lanterns] (Gyōda City website).. * Natural monument ** Maki tree - designated January 31, 1964[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/maki.html Maki tree] (Gyōda City website).. === Local information === ; Location * {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Gyōda|Q860659}} Ōaza Sakitama Aza Miyamae 5450 ; Access * From {{jalink|Gyōda Station|Q845058}} on the {{jalink|JR Takasaki Line|Q499071}}, take the {{jalink|Gyōda City Circular Bus|Q11626179}} sightseeing loop and get off at “Sakitama Shrine-mae” stop : See also “{{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds#Access|Q5017775}}” or “{{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Park#Access|Q17224997}}.” ; Nearby * {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Park|Q17224997}} ** {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}} ** {{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|Q5017775}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine history board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|1088244/495}} 'Meiji Jinja Shiryō Fuken Gōsha (Upper)'] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 495. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 11 Place Names of Saitama Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910300|ref={{SfnRef|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kenichi Tanigawa|editor-link=谷川健一|author=Reiji Harashima|authorlink=原島礼二|year=1984|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 11 Kantō|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{SfnRef|前玉神社（神々）|1984年}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}} * {{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakitama-jinja}} * [http://sakitama-jinja.com/index.html Musashi Province Sakitama Shrine] - official site {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:行田市の建築物]] [[Category:行田市の歴史]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{ill|Q11557453|lt=Q11557453|WD=Q11557453}} {{commons category|Category:Sakitama-jinja}} [[JA:前玉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11397359}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakitama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakitama-jinja, haiden.JPG|250px]]
Approach and worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Gyōda|Q860659}}{{jalink|Ōaza|Q424857}} Sakitama {{jalink|Koaza|Q5327509|Aza}} Miyamae 5450 |位置 = {{Coord|36|7|23.5|N|139|28|46.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto
Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (2 minor seats)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Sakitama Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|April 15|Q2497}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=7|lat_sec=23.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=28|lon_sec=46.7|label=Sakitama Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{Coord|36|7|23.5|N|139|28|46.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Sengen-zuka Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|{{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}} where Sakitama Shrine is enshrined}}]] '''Sakitama Shrine''' (さきたまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sakitama, {{jalink|Gyōda|Q860659}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534|shrine rank}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. The shrine name “Sakitama” is said to be the origin of the place name “Saitama”Shrine history board.. It stands within the {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}} in northern Saitama Prefecture, and the shrine itself is on top of {{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following two. * '''{{jalink|Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Sakitamahime|Q0|Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto}}''' : In the '{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}},' regarding Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto, it is written that she is a daughter of {{jalink|Ame-no-Mikushi-no-Kami}} and consort of {{jalink|Hayamikatake-sawayajinu-mi-no-Kami}}, a descendant of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-Ōkami}} (there is no description regarding Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto). === History === === Founding === The founding is unknown. Sakitama Shrine stands within the {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}}, and its main sanctuary also faces the direction of the kofun cluster. The ruling stratum of these kofun is estimated to have been the ancient ritual group of Sakitama Shrine{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}, and one theory infers that worship began in the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}} as protection of the kofun cluster. The origin of the shrine name “Sakitama” is also unclear, but because the family register ledger “Yamashiro no Kuni Atago-gun Kumoshimo no Sato Keichō” (in the '{{jalink|Shōsōin Documents|Q11545362}}') from {{jalink|Jinki|Q1373210}} 3 ({{jalink|726|Q23555}}) already shows the notation “Musashi no Kuni Sakitama-gun,” this “Sakitama” is thought to have later changed to “Saitama (sakitama/saitama)”. === Overview history === In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is recorded in {{jalink|Sakitama District|Q11427160}} of {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}} as “Sakitama Shrine, two seats,” and both seats are listed as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Both readings “Sakitama” and “Saitama” are annotated. Movements in the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}} are unknown{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}, but according to shrine tradition, after inviting an Asama shrine from {{jalink|Oshi Castle|Q1074393}}, it took the name “Asama Shrine,” and the kofun where it stands was regarded as Mt. Fuji{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}. It is also said that originally the shrine on top of the kofun was called “Upper Shrine” and the one on the middle slope “Lower Shrine,” and in Meiji this was fixed as “Sakitama Shrine” (upper) and “Asama Shrine” (lower){{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} in the modern shrine rank system. === Precincts === [[File:Sakitama-jinja, tourou.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{Center|Man'yō lanterns (both sides of approach)
(Gyōda City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Sakitama-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{Center|Stone torii (Gyōda City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Sakitama-jinja, maki.JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{Center|[[マキ|Maki tree]] (Gyōda City-designated Natural Monument)}}]] The main shrine buildings stand on the summit of {{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}} and consist of main sanctuary and worship hall. On both sides of the stair entrance to the summit stand two 2-meter stone lanterns called “Man'yō Lanterns.” They were reportedly dedicated by parishioners of Sakitama Shrine on October 15, {{jalink|1697|Q7702}} ({{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} 10) to commemorate fulfillment of prayers. Each lantern bears one poem from the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' related to this area. {{Cquote| '''Sakitama no Tsu''' (Vol.14, No.3380, anonymous) * Man'yōgana text : 佐吉多万能　津爾乎流布禰乃　可是乎伊多美 : 都奈波多由登毛　許登奈多延曽禰 * Reading : 埼玉（さきたま）の　津に居（を）る船の　風をいたみ : 綱は絶ゆとも　言（こと）な絶えそね '''Kosaki-numa''' (Vol.9, No.1744, anonymous) * Man'yōgana text : 前玉之　小埼乃沼爾　鴨曽翼霧 : 己尾爾　零置流霜乎　掃等爾有欺 * Reading : 埼玉の　小埼の沼に　鴨そ翼（はね）霧（き）る : 己（おの）が尾に　降りおける霜を　払ふとにあらし }} These lanterns are designated cultural properties of Gyōda City. The “{{jalink|Kosaki-numa|Q18991228}}” in the poem has an identified location east of Sakitama Shrine ({{Coord|36|07|13.24|N|139|30|06.51|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|name=Kosaki-numa}}). Also, the stone torii at the precinct entrance was built in November {{jalink|1676|Q7628}} ({{jalink|Enpō|Q1145735}} 4) and is designated a cultural property of Gyōda City. The {{jalink|maki|Q117435}} tree standing near the torii is estimated at 600 years old and 16 meters tall, the largest existing maki in Saitama Prefecture, and is designated a natural monument of Gyōda City. === Sessha and massha === * Asama Shrine ** Deity: {{jalink|Konohanasakuyahime|Q80497|Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto}} *: Said to have been invited from {{jalink|Oshi Castle|Q1074393}}. Before Meiji, the main shrine on the kofun summit was called “Upper Shrine,” and Asama Shrine on the slope was called “Lower Shrine.” * Meiji Shrine : Shrines of the Sakitama district were merged in the {{jalink|Meiji period}}. Total of 16 deities. * Tenjin Shrine * Ebisu Shrine File:Sakitama-jinja, Sengen-jinja.JPG|Asama Shrine File:Sakitama-jinja, Meiji-jinja.JPG|Meiji Shrine === Festivals === List of annual festivals held at Sakitama Shrine. * {{jalink|New Year Festival}} ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * Selection rite for sacred rice field, opening rite for sacred rice field work (late March) * Kangaku Festival (early April) * {{jalink|Grand Annual Festival}} ({{jalink|April 15|Q2497}}) * Rice-planting rite (early June) * Hatsuyama ({{jalink|July 1|Q2700}}-{{jalink|July 15|Q2686|15}}) * {{jalink|Nagoshi no Ōharae}} ({{jalink|July 30|Q2716}}) * Tanabata rite (early August) * Harvest-cutting festival (early October) * Lantern Festival ({{jalink|October 15|Q2919}}) * {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}} (late October) * {{jalink|Shichi-Go-San|Q1072387}} (late October - November) * {{jalink|Hoshi Festival}} (winter solstice in December) * {{jalink|Ōharae|Q11261910}} ({{jalink|December 31|Q2912}}) * Sakitama rite (December 31) === Cultural properties === === Gyōda City-designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone torii (building) - designated March 25, 1999[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/sakitamajinjyanoisitorii.html Stone torii of Sakitama Shrine] (Gyōda City website).. ** Stone lanterns (crafts) - designated March 10, 1967[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/isidourou.html Stone lanterns] (Gyōda City website).. * Natural monument ** Maki tree - designated January 31, 1964[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/maki.html Maki tree] (Gyōda City website).. === Local information === ; Location * {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Gyōda|Q860659}} Ōaza Sakitama Aza Miyamae 5450 ; Access * From {{jalink|Gyōda Station|Q845058}} on the {{jalink|JR Takasaki Line|Q499071}}, take the {{jalink|Gyōda City Circular Bus|Q11626179}} sightseeing loop and get off at “Sakitama Shrine-mae” stop : See also “{{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds#Access|Q5017775}}” or “{{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Park#Access|Q17224997}}.” ; Nearby * {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Park|Q17224997}} ** {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}} ** {{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|Q5017775}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine history board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|1088244/495}} 'Meiji Jinja Shiryō Fuken Gōsha (Upper)'] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 495. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 11 Place Names of Saitama Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910300|ref={{SfnRef|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kenichi Tanigawa|editor-link=谷川健一|author=Reiji Harashima|authorlink=原島礼二|year=1984|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 11 Kantō|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{SfnRef|前玉神社（神々）|1984年}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}} * {{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakitama-jinja}} * [http://sakitama-jinja.com/index.html Musashi Province Sakitama Shrine] - official site {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:行田市の建築物]] [[Category:行田市の歴史]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

Approach and worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Gyōda|Q860659}}{{jalink|Ōaza|Q424857}} Sakitama {{jalink|Koaza|Q5327509|Aza}} Miyamae 5450 |位置 = {{Coord|36|7|23.5|N|139|28|46.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto
Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (2 minor seats)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Sakitama Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|April 15|Q2497}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=7|lat_sec=23.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=28|lon_sec=46.7|label=Sakitama Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{Coord|36|7|23.5|N|139|28|46.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Sengen-zuka Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|{{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}} where Sakitama Shrine is enshrined}}]] '''Sakitama Shrine''' (さきたまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sakitama, {{jalink|Gyōda|Q860659}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534|shrine rank}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. The shrine name “Sakitama” is said to be the origin of the place name “Saitama”Shrine history board.. It stands within the {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}} in northern Saitama Prefecture, and the shrine itself is on top of {{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following two. * '''{{jalink|Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Sakitamahime|Q0|Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto}}''' : In the '{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}},' regarding Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto, it is written that she is a daughter of {{jalink|Ame-no-Mikushi-no-Kami}} and consort of {{jalink|Hayamikatake-sawayajinu-mi-no-Kami}}, a descendant of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-Ōkami}} (there is no description regarding Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto). === History === === Founding === The founding is unknown. Sakitama Shrine stands within the {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}}, and its main sanctuary also faces the direction of the kofun cluster. The ruling stratum of these kofun is estimated to have been the ancient ritual group of Sakitama Shrine{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}, and one theory infers that worship began in the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}} as protection of the kofun cluster. The origin of the shrine name “Sakitama” is also unclear, but because the family register ledger “Yamashiro no Kuni Atago-gun Kumoshimo no Sato Keichō” (in the '{{jalink|Shōsōin Documents|Q11545362}}') from {{jalink|Jinki|Q1373210}} 3 ({{jalink|726|Q23555}}) already shows the notation “Musashi no Kuni Sakitama-gun,” this “Sakitama” is thought to have later changed to “Saitama (sakitama/saitama)”. === Overview history === In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is recorded in {{jalink|Sakitama District|Q11427160}} of {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}} as “Sakitama Shrine, two seats,” and both seats are listed as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Both readings “Sakitama” and “Saitama” are annotated. Movements in the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}} are unknown{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}, but according to shrine tradition, after inviting an Asama shrine from {{jalink|Oshi Castle|Q1074393}}, it took the name “Asama Shrine,” and the kofun where it stands was regarded as Mt. Fuji{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}. It is also said that originally the shrine on top of the kofun was called “Upper Shrine” and the one on the middle slope “Lower Shrine,” and in Meiji this was fixed as “Sakitama Shrine” (upper) and “Asama Shrine” (lower){{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} in the modern shrine rank system. === Precincts === [[File:Sakitama-jinja, tourou.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{Center|Man'yō lanterns (both sides of approach)
(Gyōda City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Sakitama-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{Center|Stone torii (Gyōda City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Sakitama-jinja, maki.JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{Center|[[マキ|Maki tree]] (Gyōda City-designated Natural Monument)}}]] The main shrine buildings stand on the summit of {{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}} and consist of main sanctuary and worship hall. On both sides of the stair entrance to the summit stand two 2-meter stone lanterns called “Man'yō Lanterns.” They were reportedly dedicated by parishioners of Sakitama Shrine on October 15, {{jalink|1697|Q7702}} ({{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} 10) to commemorate fulfillment of prayers. Each lantern bears one poem from the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' related to this area. {{Cquote| '''Sakitama no Tsu''' (Vol.14, No.3380, anonymous) * Man'yōgana text : 佐吉多万能　津爾乎流布禰乃　可是乎伊多美 : 都奈波多由登毛　許登奈多延曽禰 * Reading : 埼玉（さきたま）の　津に居（を）る船の　風をいたみ : 綱は絶ゆとも　言（こと）な絶えそね '''Kosaki-numa''' (Vol.9, No.1744, anonymous) * Man'yōgana text : 前玉之　小埼乃沼爾　鴨曽翼霧 : 己尾爾　零置流霜乎　掃等爾有欺 * Reading : 埼玉の　小埼の沼に　鴨そ翼（はね）霧（き）る : 己（おの）が尾に　降りおける霜を　払ふとにあらし }} These lanterns are designated cultural properties of Gyōda City. The “{{jalink|Kosaki-numa|Q18991228}}” in the poem has an identified location east of Sakitama Shrine ({{Coord|36|07|13.24|N|139|30|06.51|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|name=Kosaki-numa}}). Also, the stone torii at the precinct entrance was built in November {{jalink|1676|Q7628}} ({{jalink|Enpō|Q1145735}} 4) and is designated a cultural property of Gyōda City. The {{jalink|maki|Q117435}} tree standing near the torii is estimated at 600 years old and 16 meters tall, the largest existing maki in Saitama Prefecture, and is designated a natural monument of Gyōda City. === Sessha and massha === * Asama Shrine ** Deity: {{jalink|Konohanasakuyahime|Q80497|Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto}} *: Said to have been invited from {{jalink|Oshi Castle|Q1074393}}. Before Meiji, the main shrine on the kofun summit was called “Upper Shrine,” and Asama Shrine on the slope was called “Lower Shrine.” * Meiji Shrine : Shrines of the Sakitama district were merged in the {{jalink|Meiji period}}. Total of 16 deities. * Tenjin Shrine * Ebisu Shrine File:Sakitama-jinja, Sengen-jinja.JPG|Asama Shrine File:Sakitama-jinja, Meiji-jinja.JPG|Meiji Shrine === Festivals === List of annual festivals held at Sakitama Shrine. * {{jalink|New Year Festival}} ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * Selection rite for sacred rice field, opening rite for sacred rice field work (late March) * Kangaku Festival (early April) * {{jalink|Grand Annual Festival}} ({{jalink|April 15|Q2497}}) * Rice-planting rite (early June) * Hatsuyama ({{jalink|July 1|Q2700}}-{{jalink|July 15|Q2686|15}}) * {{jalink|Nagoshi no Ōharae}} ({{jalink|July 30|Q2716}}) * Tanabata rite (early August) * Harvest-cutting festival (early October) * Lantern Festival ({{jalink|October 15|Q2919}}) * {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}} (late October) * {{jalink|Shichi-Go-San|Q1072387}} (late October - November) * {{jalink|Hoshi Festival}} (winter solstice in December) * {{jalink|Ōharae|Q11261910}} ({{jalink|December 31|Q2912}}) * Sakitama rite (December 31) === Cultural properties === === Gyōda City-designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone torii (building) - designated March 25, 1999[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/sakitamajinjyanoisitorii.html Stone torii of Sakitama Shrine] (Gyōda City website).. ** Stone lanterns (crafts) - designated March 10, 1967[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/isidourou.html Stone lanterns] (Gyōda City website).. * Natural monument ** Maki tree - designated January 31, 1964[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/maki.html Maki tree] (Gyōda City website).. === Local information === ; Location * {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Gyōda|Q860659}} Ōaza Sakitama Aza Miyamae 5450 ; Access * From {{jalink|Gyōda Station|Q845058}} on the {{jalink|JR Takasaki Line|Q499071}}, take the {{jalink|Gyōda City Circular Bus|Q11626179}} sightseeing loop and get off at “Sakitama Shrine-mae” stop : See also “{{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds#Access|Q5017775}}” or “{{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Park#Access|Q17224997}}.” ; {{ill|Q11557453|lt=Q11557453|WD=Q11557453}} {{commons category|Category:Sakitama-jinja}} [[JA:前玉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11397359}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakitama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakitama-jinja, haiden.JPG|250px]]
Approach and worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Gyōda|Q860659}}{{jalink|Ōaza|Q424857}} Sakitama {{jalink|Koaza|Q5327509|Aza}} Miyamae 5450 |位置 = {{Coord|36|7|23.5|N|139|28|46.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto
Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (2 minor seats)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Sakitama Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|April 15|Q2497}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=7|lat_sec=23.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=28|lon_sec=46.7|label=Sakitama Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{Coord|36|7|23.5|N|139|28|46.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Sengen-zuka Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|{{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}} where Sakitama Shrine is enshrined}}]] '''Sakitama Shrine''' (さきたまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sakitama, {{jalink|Gyōda|Q860659}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534|shrine rank}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. The shrine name “Sakitama” is said to be the origin of the place name “Saitama”Shrine history board.. It stands within the {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}} in northern Saitama Prefecture, and the shrine itself is on top of {{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following two. * '''{{jalink|Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Sakitamahime|Q0|Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto}}''' : In the '{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}},' regarding Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto, it is written that she is a daughter of {{jalink|Ame-no-Mikushi-no-Kami}} and consort of {{jalink|Hayamikatake-sawayajinu-mi-no-Kami}}, a descendant of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-Ōkami}} (there is no description regarding Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto). === History === === Founding === The founding is unknown. Sakitama Shrine stands within the {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}}, and its main sanctuary also faces the direction of the kofun cluster. The ruling stratum of these kofun is estimated to have been the ancient ritual group of Sakitama Shrine{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}, and one theory infers that worship began in the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}} as protection of the kofun cluster. The origin of the shrine name “Sakitama” is also unclear, but because the family register ledger “Yamashiro no Kuni Atago-gun Kumoshimo no Sato Keichō” (in the '{{jalink|Shōsōin Documents|Q11545362}}') from {{jalink|Jinki|Q1373210}} 3 ({{jalink|726|Q23555}}) already shows the notation “Musashi no Kuni Sakitama-gun,” this “Sakitama” is thought to have later changed to “Saitama (sakitama/saitama)”. === Overview history === In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is recorded in {{jalink|Sakitama District|Q11427160}} of {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}} as “Sakitama Shrine, two seats,” and both seats are listed as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Both readings “Sakitama” and “Saitama” are annotated. Movements in the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}} are unknown{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}, but according to shrine tradition, after inviting an Asama shrine from {{jalink|Oshi Castle|Q1074393}}, it took the name “Asama Shrine,” and the kofun where it stands was regarded as Mt. Fuji{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}. It is also said that originally the shrine on top of the kofun was called “Upper Shrine” and the one on the middle slope “Lower Shrine,” and in Meiji this was fixed as “Sakitama Shrine” (upper) and “Asama Shrine” (lower){{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} in the modern shrine rank system. === Precincts === [[File:Sakitama-jinja, tourou.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{Center|Man'yō lanterns (both sides of approach)
(Gyōda City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Sakitama-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{Center|Stone torii (Gyōda City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Sakitama-jinja, maki.JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{Center|[[マキ|Maki tree]] (Gyōda City-designated Natural Monument)}}]] The main shrine buildings stand on the summit of {{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}} and consist of main sanctuary and worship hall. On both sides of the stair entrance to the summit stand two 2-meter stone lanterns called “Man'yō Lanterns.” They were reportedly dedicated by parishioners of Sakitama Shrine on October 15, {{jalink|1697|Q7702}} ({{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} 10) to commemorate fulfillment of prayers. Each lantern bears one poem from the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' related to this area. {{Cquote| '''Sakitama no Tsu''' (Vol.14, No.3380, anonymous) * Man'yōgana text : 佐吉多万能　津爾乎流布禰乃　可是乎伊多美 : 都奈波多由登毛　許登奈多延曽禰 * Reading : 埼玉（さきたま）の　津に居（を）る船の　風をいたみ : 綱は絶ゆとも　言（こと）な絶えそね '''Kosaki-numa''' (Vol.9, No.1744, anonymous) * Man'yōgana text : 前玉之　小埼乃沼爾　鴨曽翼霧 : 己尾爾　零置流霜乎　掃等爾有欺 * Reading : 埼玉の　小埼の沼に　鴨そ翼（はね）霧（き）る : 己（おの）が尾に　降りおける霜を　払ふとにあらし }} These lanterns are designated cultural properties of Gyōda City. The “{{jalink|Kosaki-numa|Q18991228}}” in the poem has an identified location east of Sakitama Shrine ({{Coord|36|07|13.24|N|139|30|06.51|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|name=Kosaki-numa}}). Also, the stone torii at the precinct entrance was built in November {{jalink|1676|Q7628}} ({{jalink|Enpō|Q1145735}} 4) and is designated a cultural property of Gyōda City. The {{jalink|maki|Q117435}} tree standing near the torii is estimated at 600 years old and 16 meters tall, the largest existing maki in Saitama Prefecture, and is designated a natural monument of Gyōda City. === Sessha and massha === * Asama Shrine ** Deity: {{jalink|Konohanasakuyahime|Q80497|Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto}} *: Said to have been invited from {{jalink|Oshi Castle|Q1074393}}. Before Meiji, the main shrine on the kofun summit was called “Upper Shrine,” and Asama Shrine on the slope was called “Lower Shrine.” * Meiji Shrine : Shrines of the Sakitama district were merged in the {{jalink|Meiji period}}. Total of 16 deities. * Tenjin Shrine * Ebisu Shrine File:Sakitama-jinja, Sengen-jinja.JPG|Asama Shrine File:Sakitama-jinja, Meiji-jinja.JPG|Meiji Shrine === Festivals === List of annual festivals held at Sakitama Shrine. * {{jalink|New Year Festival}} ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * Selection rite for sacred rice field, opening rite for sacred rice field work (late March) * Kangaku Festival (early April) * {{jalink|Grand Annual Festival}} ({{jalink|April 15|Q2497}}) * Rice-planting rite (early June) * Hatsuyama ({{jalink|July 1|Q2700}}-{{jalink|July 15|Q2686|15}}) * {{jalink|Nagoshi no Ōharae}} ({{jalink|July 30|Q2716}}) * Tanabata rite (early August) * Harvest-cutting festival (early October) * Lantern Festival ({{jalink|October 15|Q2919}}) * {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}} (late October) * {{jalink|Shichi-Go-San|Q1072387}} (late October - November) * {{jalink|Hoshi Festival}} (winter solstice in December) * {{jalink|Ōharae|Q11261910}} ({{jalink|December 31|Q2912}}) * Sakitama rite (December 31) === Cultural properties === === Gyōda City-designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone torii (building) - designated March 25, 1999[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/sakitamajinjyanoisitorii.html Stone torii of Sakitama Shrine] (Gyōda City website).. ** Stone lanterns (crafts) - designated March 10, 1967[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/isidourou.html Stone lanterns] (Gyōda City website).. * Natural monument ** Maki tree - designated January 31, 1964[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/maki.html Maki tree] (Gyōda City website).. === Local information === ; Location * {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Gyōda|Q860659}} Ōaza Sakitama Aza Miyamae 5450 ; Access * From {{jalink|Gyōda Station|Q845058}} on the {{jalink|JR Takasaki Line|Q499071}}, take the {{jalink|Gyōda City Circular Bus|Q11626179}} sightseeing loop and get off at “Sakitama Shrine-mae” stop : See also “{{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds#Access|Q5017775}}” or “{{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Park#Access|Q17224997}}.” ; Nearby * {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Park|Q17224997}} ** {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}} ** {{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|Q5017775}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine history board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|1088244/495}} 'Meiji Jinja Shiryō Fuken Gōsha (Upper)'] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 495. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 11 Place Names of Saitama Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910300|ref={{SfnRef|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kenichi Tanigawa|editor-link=谷川健一|author=Reiji Harashima|authorlink=原島礼二|year=1984|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 11 Kantō|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{SfnRef|前玉神社（神々）|1984年}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}} * {{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakitama-jinja}} * [http://sakitama-jinja.com/index.html Musashi Province Sakitama Shrine] - official site {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:行田市の建築物]] [[Category:行田市の歴史]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]]

'1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県行田市大字埼玉字宮前5450', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '埼玉県行田市埼玉', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Q11557453|lt=Q11557453|WD=Q11557453}} {{commons category|Category:Sakitama-jinja}} [[JA:前玉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11397359}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakitama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakitama-jinja, haiden.JPG|250px]]
Approach and worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Gyōda|Q860659}}{{jalink|Ōaza|Q424857}} Sakitama {{jalink|Koaza|Q5327509|Aza}} Miyamae 5450 |位置 = {{Coord|36|7|23.5|N|139|28|46.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto
Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (2 minor seats)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Sakitama Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|April 15|Q2497}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=7|lat_sec=23.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=28|lon_sec=46.7|label=Sakitama Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{Coord|36|7|23.5|N|139|28|46.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Sengen-zuka Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|{{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}} where Sakitama Shrine is enshrined}}]] '''Sakitama Shrine''' (さきたまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sakitama, {{jalink|Gyōda|Q860659}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534|shrine rank}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. The shrine name “Sakitama” is said to be the origin of the place name “Saitama”Shrine history board.. It stands within the {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}} in northern Saitama Prefecture, and the shrine itself is on top of {{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following two. * '''{{jalink|Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Sakitamahime|Q0|Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto}}''' : In the '{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}},' regarding Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto, it is written that she is a daughter of {{jalink|Ame-no-Mikushi-no-Kami}} and consort of {{jalink|Hayamikatake-sawayajinu-mi-no-Kami}}, a descendant of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-Ōkami}} (there is no description regarding Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto). === History === === Founding === The founding is unknown. Sakitama Shrine stands within the {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}}, and its main sanctuary also faces the direction of the kofun cluster. The ruling stratum of these kofun is estimated to have been the ancient ritual group of Sakitama Shrine{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}, and one theory infers that worship began in the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}} as protection of the kofun cluster. The origin of the shrine name “Sakitama” is also unclear, but because the family register ledger “Yamashiro no Kuni Atago-gun Kumoshimo no Sato Keichō” (in the '{{jalink|Shōsōin Documents|Q11545362}}') from {{jalink|Jinki|Q1373210}} 3 ({{jalink|726|Q23555}}) already shows the notation “Musashi no Kuni Sakitama-gun,” this “Sakitama” is thought to have later changed to “Saitama (sakitama/saitama)”. === Overview history === In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is recorded in {{jalink|Sakitama District|Q11427160}} of {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}} as “Sakitama Shrine, two seats,” and both seats are listed as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Both readings “Sakitama” and “Saitama” are annotated. Movements in the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}} are unknown{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}, but according to shrine tradition, after inviting an Asama shrine from {{jalink|Oshi Castle|Q1074393}}, it took the name “Asama Shrine,” and the kofun where it stands was regarded as Mt. Fuji{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}. It is also said that originally the shrine on top of the kofun was called “Upper Shrine” and the one on the middle slope “Lower Shrine,” and in Meiji this was fixed as “Sakitama Shrine” (upper) and “Asama Shrine” (lower){{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} in the modern shrine rank system. === Precincts === [[File:Sakitama-jinja, tourou.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{Center|Man'yō lanterns (both sides of approach)
(Gyōda City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Sakitama-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{Center|Stone torii (Gyōda City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Sakitama-jinja, maki.JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{Center|[[マキ|Maki tree]] (Gyōda City-designated Natural Monument)}}]] The main shrine buildings stand on the summit of {{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}} and consist of main sanctuary and worship hall. On both sides of the stair entrance to the summit stand two 2-meter stone lanterns called “Man'yō Lanterns.” They were reportedly dedicated by parishioners of Sakitama Shrine on October 15, {{jalink|1697|Q7702}} ({{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} 10) to commemorate fulfillment of prayers. Each lantern bears one poem from the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' related to this area. {{Cquote| '''Sakitama no Tsu''' (Vol.14, No.3380, anonymous) * Man'yōgana text : 佐吉多万能　津爾乎流布禰乃　可是乎伊多美 : 都奈波多由登毛　許登奈多延曽禰 * Reading : 埼玉（さきたま）の　津に居（を）る船の　風をいたみ : 綱は絶ゆとも　言（こと）な絶えそね '''Kosaki-numa''' (Vol.9, No.1744, anonymous) * Man'yōgana text : 前玉之　小埼乃沼爾　鴨曽翼霧 : 己尾爾　零置流霜乎　掃等爾有欺 * Reading : 埼玉の　小埼の沼に　鴨そ翼（はね）霧（き）る : 己（おの）が尾に　降りおける霜を　払ふとにあらし }} These lanterns are designated cultural properties of Gyōda City. The “{{jalink|Kosaki-numa|Q18991228}}” in the poem has an identified location east of Sakitama Shrine ({{Coord|36|07|13.24|N|139|30|06.51|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|name=Kosaki-numa}}). Also, the stone torii at the precinct entrance was built in November {{jalink|1676|Q7628}} ({{jalink|Enpō|Q1145735}} 4) and is designated a cultural property of Gyōda City. The {{jalink|maki|Q117435}} tree standing near the torii is estimated at 600 years old and 16 meters tall, the largest existing maki in Saitama Prefecture, and is designated a natural monument of Gyōda City. === Sessha and massha === * Asama Shrine ** Deity: {{jalink|Konohanasakuyahime|Q80497|Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto}} *: Said to have been invited from {{jalink|Oshi Castle|Q1074393}}. Before Meiji, the main shrine on the kofun summit was called “Upper Shrine,” and Asama Shrine on the slope was called “Lower Shrine.” * Meiji Shrine : Shrines of the Sakitama district were merged in the {{jalink|Meiji period}}. Total of 16 deities. * Tenjin Shrine * Ebisu Shrine File:Sakitama-jinja, Sengen-jinja.JPG|Asama Shrine File:Sakitama-jinja, Meiji-jinja.JPG|Meiji Shrine === Festivals === List of annual festivals held at Sakitama Shrine. * {{jalink|New Year Festival}} ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * Selection rite for sacred rice field, opening rite for sacred rice field work (late March) * Kangaku Festival (early April) * {{jalink|Grand Annual Festival}} ({{jalink|April 15|Q2497}}) * Rice-planting rite (early June) * Hatsuyama ({{jalink|July 1|Q2700}}-{{jalink|July 15|Q2686|15}}) * {{jalink|Nagoshi no Ōharae}} ({{jalink|July 30|Q2716}}) * Tanabata rite (early August) * Harvest-cutting festival (early October) * Lantern Festival ({{jalink|October 15|Q2919}}) * {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}} (late October) * {{jalink|Shichi-Go-San|Q1072387}} (late October - November) * {{jalink|Hoshi Festival}} (winter solstice in December) * {{jalink|Ōharae|Q11261910}} ({{jalink|December 31|Q2912}}) * Sakitama rite (December 31) === Cultural properties === === Gyōda City-designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone torii (building) - designated March 25, 1999[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/sakitamajinjyanoisitorii.html Stone torii of Sakitama Shrine] (Gyōda City website).. ** Stone lanterns (crafts) - designated March 10, 1967[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/isidourou.html Stone lanterns] (Gyōda City website).. * Natural monument ** Maki tree - designated January 31, 1964[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/maki.html Maki tree] (Gyōda City website).. === Local information === ; Location * {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Gyōda|Q860659}} Ōaza Sakitama Aza Miyamae 5450 ; Access * From {{jalink|Gyōda Station|Q845058}} on the {{jalink|JR Takasaki Line|Q499071}}, take the {{jalink|Gyōda City Circular Bus|Q11626179}} sightseeing loop and get off at “Sakitama Shrine-mae” stop : See also “{{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds#Access|Q5017775}}” or “{{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Park#Access|Q17224997}}.” ; Nearby * {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Park|Q17224997}} ** {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}} ** {{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|Q5017775}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine history board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|1088244/495}} 'Meiji Jinja Shiryō Fuken Gōsha (Upper)'] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 495. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 11 Place Names of Saitama Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910300|ref={{SfnRef|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kenichi Tanigawa|editor-link=谷川健一|author=Reiji Harashima|authorlink=原島礼二|year=1984|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 11 Kantō|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{SfnRef|前玉神社（神々）|1984年}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580}} * {{jalink|Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakitama-jinja}} * [http://sakitama-jinja.com/index.html Musashi Province Sakitama Shrine] - official site {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:行田市の建築物]] [[Category:行田市の歴史]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|Q11557453|lt=Q11557453|WD=Q11557453}} {{commons category|Category:Sakitama-jinja}} [[JA:前玉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11397359}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakitama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakitama-jinja, haiden.JPG|250px]]
Approach and worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Gyōda|Q860659|WD=Q860659}}{{ill|lt=Ōaza|Q424857|WD=Q424857}} Sakitama {{ill|lt=Koaza|Q5327509|WD=Q5327509|lt=Aza}} Miyamae 5450 |位置 = {{Coord|36|7|23.5|N|139|28|46.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto
Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (2 minor seats)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Sakitama Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=7|lat_sec=23.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=28|lon_sec=46.7|label=Sakitama Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{Coord|36|7|23.5|N|139|28|46.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Sengen-zuka Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|{{ill|lt=Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453|WD=Q11557453}} where Sakitama Shrine is enshrined}}]] '''Sakitama Shrine''' (さきたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sakitama, {{ill|lt=Gyōda|Q860659|WD=Q860659}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. The shrine name “Sakitama” is said to be the origin of the place name “Saitama”Shrine history board.. It stands within the {{ill|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580|WD=Q2213580}} in northern Saitama Prefecture, and the shrine itself is on top of {{ill|lt=Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453|WD=Q11557453}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following two. * '''{{ill||lt=Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Sakitamahime|Q0|WD=Q0|lt=Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto}}''' : In the '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}},' regarding Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto, it is written that she is a daughter of {{ill||lt=Ame-no-Mikushi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} and consort of {{ill||lt=Hayamikatake-sawayajinu-mi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, a descendant of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-Ōkami}} (there is no description regarding Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto). === History === === Founding === The founding is unknown. Sakitama Shrine stands within the {{ill|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580|WD=Q2213580}}, and its main sanctuary also faces the direction of the kofun cluster. The ruling stratum of these kofun is estimated to have been the ancient ritual group of Sakitama Shrine{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}, and one theory infers that worship began in the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}} as protection of the kofun cluster. The origin of the shrine name “Sakitama” is also unclear, but because the family register ledger “Yamashiro no Kuni Atago-gun Kumoshimo no Sato Keichō” (in the '{{ill|lt=Shōsōin Documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362}}') from {{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210}} 3 ({{ill|lt=726|Q23555|WD=Q23555}}) already shows the notation “Musashi no Kuni Sakitama-gun,” this “Sakitama” is thought to have later changed to “Saitama (sakitama/saitama)”. === Overview history === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), it is recorded in {{ill|lt=Sakitama District|Q11427160|WD=Q11427160}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} as “Sakitama Shrine, two seats,” and both seats are listed as {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. Both readings “Sakitama” and “Saitama” are annotated. Movements in the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} are unknown{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}, but according to shrine tradition, after inviting an Asama shrine from {{ill|lt=Oshi Castle|Q1074393|WD=Q1074393}}, it took the name “Asama Shrine,” and the kofun where it stands was regarded as Mt. Fuji{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}. It is also said that originally the shrine on top of the kofun was called “Upper Shrine” and the one on the middle slope “Lower Shrine,” and in Meiji this was fixed as “Sakitama Shrine” (upper) and “Asama Shrine” (lower){{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in the modern shrine rank system. === Precincts === [[File:Sakitama-jinja, tourou.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{Center|Man'yō lanterns (both sides of approach)
(Gyōda City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Sakitama-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{Center|Stone torii (Gyōda City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Sakitama-jinja, maki.JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{Center|[[マキ|Maki tree]] (Gyōda City-designated Natural Monument)}}]] The main shrine buildings stand on the summit of {{ill|lt=Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453|WD=Q11557453}} and consist of main sanctuary and worship hall. On both sides of the stair entrance to the summit stand two 2-meter stone lanterns called “Man'yō Lanterns.” They were reportedly dedicated by parishioners of Sakitama Shrine on October 15, {{ill|lt=1697|Q7702|WD=Q7702}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 10) to commemorate fulfillment of prayers. Each lantern bears one poem from the '{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321}}' related to this area. {{Cquote| '''Sakitama no Tsu''' (Vol.14, No.3380, anonymous) * Man'yōgana text : 佐吉多万能　津爾乎流布禰乃　可是乎伊多美 : 都奈波多由登毛　許登奈多延曽禰 * Reading : 埼玉（さきたま）の　津に居（を）る船の　風をいたみ : 綱は絶ゆとも　言（こと）な絶えそね '''Kosaki-numa''' (Vol.9, No.1744, anonymous) * Man'yōgana text : 前玉之　小埼乃沼爾　鴨曽翼霧 : 己尾爾　零置流霜乎　掃等爾有欺 * Reading : 埼玉の　小埼の沼に　鴨そ翼（はね）霧（き）る : 己（おの）が尾に　降りおける霜を　払ふとにあらし }} These lanterns are designated cultural properties of Gyōda City. The “{{ill|lt=Kosaki-numa|Q18991228|WD=Q18991228}}” in the poem has an identified location east of Sakitama Shrine ({{Coord|36|07|13.24|N|139|30|06.51|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|name=Kosaki-numa}}). Also, the stone torii at the precinct entrance was built in November {{ill|lt=1676|Q7628|WD=Q7628}} ({{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} 4) and is designated a cultural property of Gyōda City. The {{ill|lt=maki|Q117435|WD=Q117435}} tree standing near the torii is estimated at 600 years old and 16 meters tall, the largest existing maki in Saitama Prefecture, and is designated a natural monument of Gyōda City. === Sessha and massha === * Asama Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q80497|WD=Q80497|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto}} *: Said to have been invited from {{ill|lt=Oshi Castle|Q1074393|WD=Q1074393}}. Before Meiji, the main shrine on the kofun summit was called “Upper Shrine,” and Asama Shrine on the slope was called “Lower Shrine.” * Meiji Shrine : Shrines of the Sakitama district were merged in the {{ill||lt=Meiji period|UNKNOWN}}. Total of 16 deities. * Tenjin Shrine * Ebisu Shrine File:Sakitama-jinja, Sengen-jinja.JPG|Asama Shrine File:Sakitama-jinja, Meiji-jinja.JPG|Meiji Shrine === Festivals === List of annual festivals held at Sakitama Shrine. * {{ill||lt=New Year Festival|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) * Selection rite for sacred rice field, opening rite for sacred rice field work (late March) * Kangaku Festival (early April) * {{ill||lt=Grand Annual Festival|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}) * Rice-planting rite (early June) * Hatsuyama ({{ill|lt=July 1|Q2700|WD=Q2700}}-{{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686|lt=15}}) * {{ill||lt=Nagoshi no Ōharae|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=July 30|Q2716|WD=Q2716}}) * Tanabata rite (early August) * Harvest-cutting festival (early October) * Lantern Festival ({{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919}}) * {{ill|lt=Niiname Festival|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} (late October) * {{ill|lt=Shichi-Go-San|Q1072387|WD=Q1072387}} (late October - November) * {{ill||lt=Hoshi Festival|UNKNOWN}} (winter solstice in December) * {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910}} ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) * Sakitama rite (December 31) === Cultural properties === === Gyōda City-designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone torii (building) - designated March 25, 1999[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/sakitamajinjyanoisitorii.html Stone torii of Sakitama Shrine] (Gyōda City website).. ** Stone lanterns (crafts) - designated March 10, 1967[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/isidourou.html Stone lanterns] (Gyōda City website).. * Natural monument ** Maki tree - designated January 31, 1964[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/maki.html Maki tree] (Gyōda City website).. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Gyōda|Q860659|WD=Q860659}} Ōaza Sakitama Aza Miyamae 5450 ; Access * From {{ill|lt=Gyōda Station|Q845058|WD=Q845058}} on the {{ill|lt=JR Takasaki Line|Q499071|WD=Q499071}}, take the {{ill|lt=Gyōda City Circular Bus|Q11626179|WD=Q11626179}} sightseeing loop and get off at “Sakitama Shrine-mae” stop : See also “{{ill|lt=Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds#Access|Q5017775|WD=Q5017775}}” or “{{ill|lt=Sakitama Kofun Park#Access|Q17224997|WD=Q17224997}}.” ; Nearby * {{ill|lt=Sakitama Kofun Park|Q17224997|WD=Q17224997}} ** {{ill|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580|WD=Q2213580}} ** {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|Q5017775|WD=Q5017775}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine history board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|1088244/495}} 'Meiji Jinja Shiryō Fuken Gōsha (Upper)'] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 495. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 11 Place Names of Saitama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910300|ref={{SfnRef|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kenichi Tanigawa|editor-link=谷川健一|author=Reiji Harashima|authorlink=原島礼二|year=1984|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 11 Kantō|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{SfnRef|前玉神社（神々）|1984年}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580|WD=Q2213580}} * {{ill|lt=Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453|WD=Q11557453}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakitama-jinja}} * [http://sakitama-jinja.com/index.html Musashi Province Sakitama Shrine] - {{ill|Q11557453|lt=Q11557453|WD=Q11557453}} {{commons category|Category:Sakitama-jinja}} [[JA:前玉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11397359}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakitama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakitama-jinja, haiden.JPG|250px]]
Approach and worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Gyōda|Q860659|WD=Q860659}}{{ill|lt=Ōaza|Q424857|WD=Q424857}} Sakitama {{ill|lt=Koaza|Q5327509|WD=Q5327509|lt=Aza}} Miyamae 5450 |位置 = {{Coord|36|7|23.5|N|139|28|46.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto
Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (2 minor seats)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Sakitama Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=7|lat_sec=23.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=28|lon_sec=46.7|label=Sakitama Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{Coord|36|7|23.5|N|139|28|46.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Sengen-zuka Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|{{ill|lt=Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453|WD=Q11557453}} where Sakitama Shrine is enshrined}}]] '''Sakitama Shrine''' (さきたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sakitama, {{ill|lt=Gyōda|Q860659|WD=Q860659}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. The shrine name “Sakitama” is said to be the origin of the place name “Saitama”Shrine history board.. It stands within the {{ill|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580|WD=Q2213580}} in northern Saitama Prefecture, and the shrine itself is on top of {{ill|lt=Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453|WD=Q11557453}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following two. * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Sakitamahime|Q0|WD=Q0|lt=Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto}}''' : In the '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}},' regarding Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto, it is written that she is a daughter of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Mikushi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} and consort of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayamikatake-sawayajinu-mi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, a descendant of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-Ōkami}} (there is no description regarding Sakitamahiko-no-Mikoto). === History === === Founding === The founding is unknown. Sakitama Shrine stands within the {{ill|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580|WD=Q2213580}}, and its main sanctuary also faces the direction of the kofun cluster. The ruling stratum of these kofun is estimated to have been the ancient ritual group of Sakitama Shrine{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}, and one theory infers that worship began in the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}} as protection of the kofun cluster. The origin of the shrine name “Sakitama” is also unclear, but because the family register ledger “Yamashiro no Kuni Atago-gun Kumoshimo no Sato Keichō” (in the '{{ill|lt=Shōsōin Documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362}}') from {{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210}} 3 ({{ill|lt=726|Q23555|WD=Q23555}}) already shows the notation “Musashi no Kuni Sakitama-gun,” this “Sakitama” is thought to have later changed to “Saitama (sakitama/saitama)”. === Overview history === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), it is recorded in {{ill|lt=Sakitama District|Q11427160|WD=Q11427160}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} as “Sakitama Shrine, two seats,” and both seats are listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. Both readings “Sakitama” and “Saitama” are annotated. Movements in the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} are unknown{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}, but according to shrine tradition, after inviting an Asama shrine from {{ill|lt=Oshi Castle|Q1074393|WD=Q1074393}}, it took the name “Asama Shrine,” and the kofun where it stands was regarded as Mt. Fuji{{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}. It is also said that originally the shrine on top of the kofun was called “Upper Shrine” and the one on the middle slope “Lower Shrine,” and in Meiji this was fixed as “Sakitama Shrine” (upper) and “Asama Shrine” (lower){{Sfn|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in the modern shrine rank system. === Precincts === [[File:Sakitama-jinja, tourou.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{Center|Man'yō lanterns (both sides of approach)
(Gyōda City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Sakitama-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{Center|Stone torii (Gyōda City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Sakitama-jinja, maki.JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{Center|[[マキ|Maki tree]] (Gyōda City-designated Natural Monument)}}]] The main shrine buildings stand on the summit of {{ill|lt=Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453|WD=Q11557453}} and consist of main sanctuary and worship hall. On both sides of the stair entrance to the summit stand two 2-meter stone lanterns called “Man'yō Lanterns.” They were reportedly dedicated by parishioners of Sakitama Shrine on October 15, {{ill|lt=1697|Q7702|WD=Q7702}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 10) to commemorate fulfillment of prayers. Each lantern bears one poem from the '{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321}}' related to this area. {{Cquote| '''Sakitama no Tsu''' (Vol.14, No.3380, anonymous) * Man'yōgana text : 佐吉多万能　津爾乎流布禰乃　可是乎伊多美 : 都奈波多由登毛　許登奈多延曽禰 * Reading : 埼玉（さきたま）の　津に居（を）る船の　風をいたみ : 綱は絶ゆとも　言（こと）な絶えそね '''Kosaki-numa''' (Vol.9, No.1744, anonymous) * Man'yōgana text : 前玉之　小埼乃沼爾　鴨曽翼霧 : 己尾爾　零置流霜乎　掃等爾有欺 * Reading : 埼玉の　小埼の沼に　鴨そ翼（はね）霧（き）る : 己（おの）が尾に　降りおける霜を　払ふとにあらし }} These lanterns are designated cultural properties of Gyōda City. The “{{ill|lt=Kosaki-numa|Q18991228|WD=Q18991228}}” in the poem has an identified location east of Sakitama Shrine ({{Coord|36|07|13.24|N|139|30|06.51|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|name=Kosaki-numa}}). Also, the stone torii at the precinct entrance was built in November {{ill|lt=1676|Q7628|WD=Q7628}} ({{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} 4) and is designated a cultural property of Gyōda City. The {{ill|lt=maki|Q117435|WD=Q117435}} tree standing near the torii is estimated at 600 years old and 16 meters tall, the largest existing maki in Saitama Prefecture, and is designated a natural monument of Gyōda City. === Sessha and massha === * Asama Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q80497|WD=Q80497|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto}} *: Said to have been invited from {{ill|lt=Oshi Castle|Q1074393|WD=Q1074393}}. Before Meiji, the main shrine on the kofun summit was called “Upper Shrine,” and Asama Shrine on the slope was called “Lower Shrine.” * Meiji Shrine : Shrines of the Sakitama district were merged in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|UNKNOWN}}. Total of 16 deities. * Tenjin Shrine * Ebisu Shrine File:Sakitama-jinja, Sengen-jinja.JPG|Asama Shrine File:Sakitama-jinja, Meiji-jinja.JPG|Meiji Shrine === Festivals === List of annual festivals held at Sakitama Shrine. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=New Year Festival|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) * Selection rite for sacred rice field, opening rite for sacred rice field work (late March) * Kangaku Festival (early April) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Grand Annual Festival|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}) * Rice-planting rite (early June) * Hatsuyama ({{ill|lt=July 1|Q2700|WD=Q2700}}-{{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686|lt=15}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagoshi no Ōharae|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=July 30|Q2716|WD=Q2716}}) * Tanabata rite (early August) * Harvest-cutting festival (early October) * Lantern Festival ({{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919}}) * {{ill|lt=Niiname Festival|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} (late October) * {{ill|lt=Shichi-Go-San|Q1072387|WD=Q1072387}} (late October - November) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hoshi Festival|UNKNOWN}} (winter solstice in December) * {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910}} ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) * Sakitama rite (December 31) === Cultural properties === === Gyōda City-designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone torii (building) - designated March 25, 1999[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/sakitamajinjyanoisitorii.html Stone torii of Sakitama Shrine] (Gyōda City website).. ** Stone lanterns (crafts) - designated March 10, 1967[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/isidourou.html Stone lanterns] (Gyōda City website).. * Natural monument ** Maki tree - designated January 31, 1964[http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/maki.html Maki tree] (Gyōda City website).. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Gyōda|Q860659|WD=Q860659}} Ōaza Sakitama Aza Miyamae 5450 ; Access * From {{ill|lt=Gyōda Station|Q845058|WD=Q845058}} on the {{ill|lt=JR Takasaki Line|Q499071|WD=Q499071}}, take the {{ill|lt=Gyōda City Circular Bus|Q11626179|WD=Q11626179}} sightseeing loop and get off at “Sakitama Shrine-mae” stop : See also “{{ill|lt=Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds#Access|Q5017775|WD=Q5017775}}” or “{{ill|lt=Sakitama Kofun Park#Access|Q17224997|WD=Q17224997}}.” ; Nearby * {{ill|lt=Sakitama Kofun Park|Q17224997|WD=Q17224997}} ** {{ill|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580|WD=Q2213580}} ** {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|Q5017775|WD=Q5017775}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine history board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|1088244/495}} 'Meiji Jinja Shiryō Fuken Gōsha (Upper)'] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 495. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 11 Place Names of Saitama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910300|ref={{SfnRef|前玉神社（平凡社）|2004年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kenichi Tanigawa|editor-link=谷川健一|author=Reiji Harashima|authorlink=原島礼二|year=1984|chapter=Sakitama Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 11 Kantō|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{SfnRef|前玉神社（神々）|1984年}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Q2213580|WD=Q2213580}} * {{ill|lt=Sengen-zuka Kofun|Q11557453|WD=Q11557453}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakitama-jinja}} * [http://sakitama-jinja.com/index.html Musashi Province Sakitama Shrine] -
